source.title: Night Haven Lab setup guide - defend all four sites (Rainbow Six Siege)
source.notes: Auto-generated YouTube captions with heavy ASR noise on room/gadget names ('breach' rendered as 'brereech', 'Kaid' as 'zombie'/'zomby' near the rafters keyboard-barrier section which is corrected in context to Kaid's Electroclaw, 'Kiba Barrier' rendered as 'keyboard barrier'/'zombie keeper barrier', 'Capitao' rendered as 'capau'). Generic ranked setups, explicitly not pro-league lineups, recorded ahead of a cosmetic-only map modernization (creator states the layout itself will not change). Room/callout names are the creator's informal in-video callouts, not verified wiki callouts.

reinforcements.note: Must-reinforce. Electrify with Bandit, Kaid, or Mute if possible.

Reinforce so the Server player can dive back onto site if the Connector wall opens and that player is under heavy pressure. Keep one reinforcement spare (creator keeps a spare in the top-left of this area).

reinforcements.location: Right-hand side of the Server wall
reinforcements.note: Reinforce.
Reinforce both; attackers (e.g. Ash) will try to open the window and destroy nearby Bandit batteries, so electrify this too.
Reinforce; pair with head holes on the right wall.

reinforcements.location: Command Center triple wall (behind pillar, back of site)
reinforcements.note: Reinforced; head holes line up cleanly across it. Creator deliberately does NOT do the 'reinforce left, reinforce right, rotate middle' pro-league version of this wall — says that gets you domed from the door outside unless you're at a high rank; recommends head holes across the whole thing instead for generic ranked play.
Reinforce; pair with feet holes.

headHoles.location: Command Center right wall (paired with left wall reinforcement)
headHoles.note: See the breach and Connector through these if needed.
Lined up across the reinforced triple wall instead of a middle rotate hole — the generic-rank recommendation instead of the pro-league rotate version.

headHoles.location: Command Center right wall (near door to top of stairs)
headHoles.note: Don't stand right up against this reinforcement or you'll get domed from the door or elsewhere — hang back a little.

rotates.location: Vault rotate near Command Center / Rafters
rotates.note: Only make this if someone is actually playing Rafters (e.g. an Azami holding it) — otherwise it just hands attackers a free way onto site. Lets the Rafters player dive back in under pressure.

gadgetPlacements.note: Electrify the two reinforced Connector walls with whichever of these you have.
Feet holes catch anyone jumping in through the window above before they see you — see feet, shoot heads.
Stand at a specific spot and a C4 can be lobbed over the window and land outside; a prox alarm won't work the same way here since its throw arc is much straighter than C4's looping arc.
Electrify this wall too, same reasoning as Connector.

gadgetPlacements.location: Rafters, handrail in the middle (not the edge, not the far side)
gadgetPlacements.note: Kiba Barrier placed on this specific middle/darker section of the handrail sends the barrier up in a way that lets you peek around both sides of it. Described as a very strong barrier placement, but Rafters is an unforgiving spot that needs an experienced gunner.

gadgetPlacements.location: Rafters, higher up near the window
gadgetPlacements.note: Second Kiba Barrier placement, higher up, so you can see feet of anyone jumping in through the window.

operatorRecommendations.note: Whichever you have available should electrify the Connector and Electrical walls.
Needs to be an experienced/confident gunner. Runs the two Kiba Barrier placements on the handrail. Only make the vault rotate near Command Center if Azami (or whoever) is actually holding Rafters.

generalTips.note: Connector is an area you must actively defend early — get a drone or two on the attackers pushing through the door, apply a little pressure, but don't die here unless it's a free kill. If the Connector defender dies, attackers can be on Connector within roughly 30-50 seconds, which is very bad with only ~2 minutes left in the round.
Once the Connector wall is open, don't stand or peek anywhere along the long sightline down to the far cover point — there's an outside roof angle that sees all the way through this line.
Electrical needs a dedicated player to hold the jump-in; no other setup really needed there.
Watch for a Maverick torching a line across the bottom of the Electrical/Server wall from the steps outside, giving attackers a rafters-area angle.
The door near Server needs a prox alarm or similar; don't overcommit defending it, but expect to get peeked from the nearby corner if you sit there too long.
This whole top floor site and Connector specifically are considered the most important areas of the top floor to hold.
'Fish Stairs' (also called Blue/Water Stairs) needs a prox alarm, Kapkan mine, or similar — attackers who walk in unnoticed here get a free pick on defenders holding the Command Center head holes.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce all three basement hatches (Cargo, Animus, Exo/Exo Suit).
One of the three reinforced hatches.
One of the three reinforced hatches.
Reinforce.
Two reinforcements on this wall, watching the default plant spot.

Reinforce. Creator prefers reinforcing the left wall over the right because defenders on the left side can still cross to reinforce/hold the right wall too, whereas defenders on the right side get cut off from Animus by head holes if the wall is flipped.
Reinforce (paired with head holes on the left of this same area).

headHoles.note: 100% head holes here (not reinforced) to hold the bottom of the Exo Suit stairs.

headHoles.location: Single wall near Nano, top-right corner
headHoles.note: Covers the default plant spot reached by tucking in from Animus.
See the top of the Cargo stairs; pair with feet holes for anyone walking down the stairs from Exo, and to see feet when attackers drop the nearby hatch.

headHoles.location: Hole near Animus breach (window jump spot)
headHoles.note: Made in the floor for the window-jump angle. Deliberately placed tight to the wall, away from the window, so attackers can't see the hole before jumping in and get a free surprise pick from below (usually with a shotgun).

hatches.note: Reinforced. There's no vert directly above the Animus breach, which makes Bandit-tricking this area relatively safe.

gadgetPlacements.location: Left of the Exo hatch, hole in the wall
gadgetPlacements.note: Roughly 40% of the time attackers jump through the nearby window here — holding below with a shotgun is a near-free kill. Make the hole tight to the wall so attackers can't spot it before jumping.
Same technique but riskier since most attackers throw a Ram/breaching drone through here instead of jumping. Still worth making tight to the wall for the same reasons.
See anyone walking in through the door; also watch the side of the tank. Attackers gain nothing from these holes, so always make them.
Someone needs to hold this position and also cover the area above it (soft ceiling) or you'll get pushed out entirely.
Place a Proximity Alarm or goo mine (e.g. Kapkan/Ela-style trap) here so you get audio warning the moment attackers push onto the default plant.

gadgetPlacements.location: Hole to counter the Animus vert-and-hatch attack
gadgetPlacements.note: If attackers take the vert above Animus and drop the hatch to plant, the only counter is this specific hole — throw a C4 or smoke through it to disrupt the plant in the corridor below.

Someone needs to hold upstairs above the basement — this is where all the vert for Exo, Assembly, and Animus comes from. If attackers open it up (typically with Ram) unopposed, no one can hold the rooms below.

operatorRecommendations.note: Needs to actively waste attacker time upstairs above the basement site to stop them freely opening vert onto Exo, Assembly, and Animus.
Bandit-tricking is very feasible in Animus since there's no vert directly above the breach there — relatively safe compared to elsewhere on this site.

generalTips.note: This is a strong, dangerous attacker path: take the Animus vert, then open the hatch (no breach needed), come through, and plant with smokes covering both doors. Creator describes this being run successfully with a Capitao holding the vert and flaming both doors — the only counter is the dedicated C4/smoke hole into the corridor.
All of the ceiling area above Exo/Assembly/Animus is soft floor — a coordinated stack that takes this vert can deny defenders from standing almost anywhere below.
Creator names this their favourite map and specifically enjoys playing this basement site.

reinforcements.location: Two walls near the main Lobby breach
reinforcements.note: Reinforce; pair with head holes on one and a rotate on the other (reinforcement on the right, rotate on the left).

Reinforced at the start of the round from the stairs position outside site. No known counter to this reinforcement at time of recording, though the creator mentions hearing someone found a Capitao bolt that can stop it.
Reinforce this wall to hold Games/Lounge; two reinforcements are left spare if you'd rather not use them here.
Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Connector (upstairs mirror of the top floor Connector)
reinforcements.note: Reinforced, same as the top-floor Connector walls.

headHoles.location: Wall facing the main Lobby breach
headHoles.note: Throw C4s and smokes through here onto the breach to delay pushes; a lot of the pressure on this site comes through this breach since the only thing on the other side is a Lobby door.

headHoles.location: Second wall near the breach (paired with reinforcement/rotate)
headHoles.note: Head holes for C4s toward the door and again toward the breach, and for smoking the breach out.

headHoles.location: Head holes already covered near the breach
headHoles.note: Main counter to attackers once they control the breach: use C4s and smokes through these to deny the push, though you'll typically need several smokes plus a C4 or two to hold it off.

rotates.location: Small rotate near the stairs outside site
rotates.note: Optional personal preference rotate so the player holding outside on the stairs can come back onto site.

rotates.location: Wall near the Lobby breach (left side)
rotates.note: Paired with the reinforcement on the right side of the same wall area.

rotates.location: Upstairs, same rotate as top-floor Connector
rotates.note: Reused rotate structure from the top floor Connector setup.

gadgetPlacements.note: Feet holes all the way along; do not reinforce this wall.
Someone should start outside here holding the stairs; watch the Kitchen window and drop notifications from here, and fall back through toward the breach once pressured if the hatch is still closed.
Castle can barricade both doors here (this door, and this door) and play the room fairly safely.
Feet holes covering both windows for anyone jumping in; also watch the bottom of the stairs (can vault over but not shoot over the gap there).

operatorRecommendations.note: Barricades both doors into Games/Lounge and can hold the room safely; also peek down toward the corridor and the breach direction from here once set up.

generalTips.note: Creator ranks this as generally the third most common site seen in ranked play, though says Control/Storage (site 4) is actually the stronger/easier site to defend if your team is coordinated enough.

Attackers get very easy, early control of the area around the main Lobby breach since the only thing on the far side of it is a Lobby door — this makes early pressure into the Kitchen corridor and Kitchen itself easy for them.

Once the vert is made upstairs (all the floor above Kitchen/Meeting is soft), defenders get pushed further and further back — there is no strong way to fully stop a determined vert push once it starts.
A common attacker plant pattern once the breach is open: smoke off one specific angle, then dive up and plant near the breach — countered mainly by the C4/smoke head holes already listed.
Creator considers this a tough site to hold overall and personally rates Control/Storage as the better site, but notes Kitchen may suit teams that aren't in a full coordinated stack.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce.
Reinforce a single wall out into Lounge/Games.
Reinforce both.
Reinforce both, same pattern as the top-floor Command Center setup.
Reinforce, mirroring the top-floor Command Center triple wall treatment.

reinforcements.location: Upstairs wall toward Fish Stairs, right-hand side
reinforcements.note: Reinforce.

headHoles.note: Situational alternative: if you think you can hold Lounge/Games, open this wall and make head holes (or a rotate) instead of leaving it, then defend from Storage and peek through as needed.
Head holes toward the breach, same as the top-floor Command Center setup.

rotates.note: Rotate here, not on the left — the left side of this wall can be held/watched from a window way outside, which would make the rotate unusable.
Optional: if committing to holding Lounge/Games, add a rotate back into this room since there's otherwise only one door (through Storage) back in if Storage is lost.

hatches.note: Opened (not reinforced) purely as an escape route — you don't need to worry about attackers coming up through it (would require a Mario-style vertical play that essentially doesn't happen).

gadgetPlacements.location: Rotate window near back of Control
gadgetPlacements.note: Watch out for this window — commonly held by attackers, which is why the rotate goes on the right instead of the left.
Barricade this door in addition to reinforcing the external walls.
Hold upstairs actively — this whole area is soft floor above Control (mirrors the vert made from the basement attack path), so an unheld upstairs lets attackers jump in and plant almost freely.

operatorRecommendations.note: Option if committing to holding Lounge/Games rather than leaving the connecting wall alone.

generalTips.note: Creator considers this the best/strongest site on the map, comparable to Open Area on Bank or Bar on Chalet/Consulate-style good-but-coordination-dependent sites — it requires full 5-stack coordination to defend well.
Upstairs here is defended almost identically to the top-floor Command Center/Connector site — same wall and head hole pattern reused.
Watch for attackers sledging/bucking out the single Animus-facing wall downstairs and just walking up the stairs to flank into this site.
Creator says they'd now lean toward reinforcing to support holding Lounge/Games rather than leaving that wall alone, depending on rank/ELO.
